title: Ultraviolet Radiation. Ultraviolet is categorized as a complete carcinogen because it plays a role in every stage of malignant transformation of skin cells and skin cancer progression. Ultraviolet exerts a complex effect on human skin health. Ultraviolet radiation is molecularly and epidemiologically linked to skin cancer. However, ultraviolet also mediates the natural synthesis of vitamin D and chromophores in the skin, benefiting human skin health.

sentences.text: Ultraviolet is categorized as a complete carcinogen because it plays a role in every stage of malignant transformation of skin cells and skin cancer progression.
Ultraviolet exerts a complex effect on human skin health.
Ultraviolet radiation is molecularly and epidemiologically linked to skin cancer.
However, ultraviolet also mediates the natural synthesis of vitamin D and chromophores in the skin, benefiting human skin health.

The ultraviolet spectrum is subdivided into three categories: ultraviolet A, ultraviolet B, and ultraviolet C. Of these three subcategories, only two actually reach the earth's surface: ultraviolet A and ultraviolet B. Ultraviolet C is mostly absorbed by the ozone layer of the atmosphere.
The wavelength of ultraviolet A ranges from 320 to 400 nanometers and comprises 90 to 95 percent of the ultraviolet radiation that reaches the earth.
Ultraviolet A penetrates deeper into the skin than ultraviolet B, approximately 1000 micrometers into the epidermis and dermis.
The wavelength of ultraviolet B ranges from 290 to 320 nanometers and comprises only 5 percent of ultraviolet radiation that reaches the earth.
Ultraviolet B can penetrate 160 to 180 micrometers into the epidermis.
